llcFilesWith: Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA), Corporations, Securities & Commercial Licensing Bureau (Certificate of Assumed Name, Form CSCL/CD-541, MCL 450.4206)
soleProprietorFilesWith: County clerk of each county where the business is conducted (sole proprietors and general partnerships file an assumed name / co-partnership certificate under the county assumed name law)
feeBasis: The LARA fee for an LLC's Certificate of Assumed Name is $25. Sole proprietors pay the county clerk's fee, which varies by county.
duration: Through December 31 of the fifth full calendar year after filing
renewalRule: An LLC's assumed name expires on December 31 of the fifth full calendar year after filing and may be extended for further five-year periods by filing a renewal no earlier than 90 days before expiration.
protectionNote: An assumed name lets a company trade under a different name; it is disclosure, and although it is part of the LARA name screen, it is not a trademark and grants no exclusive market right.
searchNote: Assumed names filed by LLCs and corporations appear in the LARA business entity search. County-level assumed names for sole proprietors sit in county clerk records.
notes: Michigan splits its DBA system. An LLC files a Certificate of Assumed Name with LARA ($25, MCL 450.4206); the assumed name expires on December 31 of the fifth full calendar year after filing and renews for five-year periods. Sole proprietors and general partnerships instead file an assumed name with the county clerk. The LARA assumed name is part of the entity-name screen, so it can block a later LLC name, but it grants no exclusive right and needs no publication.

sources.title: Michigan Limited Liability Company Act — Transacting business under assumed name
Certificate of Assumed Name (Form CSCL/CD-541, $25, expires Dec 31 of the 5th year)
